channel.title: Comments for page "Nameless Bodies, in Unremembered Rooms"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Nameless Bodies, in Unremembered Rooms"

item.title: Re: Everyone still in this building right now, dead before the dawn!
item.content:encoded:
You know, I didn't realize that lyric also pretty much sums up the situation.

I wasn't actually aware any tales like this, but that might be my lack of awareness than any other factor. There's a lot of them, and it's hard to search them effectively.

I definitely see what you mean with the disparate documents - I was actually a little afraid that it might be confusing to the reader. If people like the format, I'll try to write something a bit more intricate.

item.title: Everyone still in this building right now, dead before the dawn!
item.content:encoded:
Post-"everything is fucked" scenarios from the viewpoint of some minor nobody in the Foundation are not really that unique or new. Beyond the touch of ambiguity at the end of the tale, nothing about the premise really grabbed me.

The prose was pretty enjoyable, with suggestions of the personalities of the various speakers popping up here and there. The documents and recalled conversations were a nice touch in implying a story, but I feel like just using it twice was a bit of a let-down. Still, if "I wish there was more of it" is one of my chief complaints, that's a good thing.

Honestly, I probably would have passed by this without too much notice had it not been for the title. It seems like there's a disproportionate number of tales floating around with similar title themes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome. It's interesting to see an attack from the perspective of people trying to survive the aftermath.

item.title: Nameless Bodies, in Unremembered Rooms
item.content:encoded:
A tale about the little people in the Foundation when push comes to shove.

Thanks to Sax, nyka, Recursive and others who helped in this drafting phase.

Bonus points to anyone who knows where the title is from.
